citations.note: The Licensor grants the right to 'copy, modify, create derivative works, redistribute, and make non-production use of the Licensed Work'; production use is not granted by default.
Licensor may make an 'Additional Use Grant' permitting limited production use (the carve-out is per-deployment and is set by the Licensor, not the license).

Effective on the Change Date (or the fourth anniversary of first publicly available distribution, whichever comes first) the rights granted by the specified Change License kick in and the non-production restriction terminates.
If your use of the Licensed Work does not comply with the requirements, 'you must purchase a commercial license from the Licensor ... or you must refrain from using the Licensed Work.'

external_references.source: OSI blog: The Business Source License
external_references.summary: OSI explains that BUSL permits only non-production use until the Change Date, at which point the license converts to the specified Change License (often GPLv2+). This is why BUSL is classified as source-available, not open source.

permissions.commentary: Commercial/production use is restricted during the Business Source period. Non-production use (development, test, internal evaluation) is freely permitted; production use requires either an Additional Use Grant set by the Licensor or a separately purchased commercial license. On the Change Date (no later than four years after first release) those restrictions lift and the Change License (typically GPLv2+ or a GPL-compatible license) applies instead.

citations.note: Explicit grant to 'copy, modify, create derivative works, redistribute' the Licensed Work.

permissions.commentary: Modification is permitted, but downstream use of the modified work remains bound by the same non-production restriction during the Business Source period (see s-8).

citations.note: Explicit grant to 'redistribute' the Licensed Work.
'All copies of the original and modified Licensed Work, and derivative works of the Licensed Work, are subject to this License.'

permissions.commentary: Redistribution is permitted but the same non-production restriction follows the work (s-8). Recipients can only use it non-production until the Change Date.

citations.note: Non-production use is explicitly granted; private development and evaluation fall inside this grant.

permissions.commentary: BUSL 1.1 makes no mention of patents and grants no express patent license. After the Change Date the Change License (if GPLv3-compatible) may supply one.

citations.note: 'You must conspicuously display this License on each original or modified copy of the Licensed Work.'
License terms travel with copies received from third parties.

conditions.commentary: BUSL is source-available by convention (the Licensor must publish the source to offer BUSL terms), but the license text imposes no source-disclosure obligation on licensees. After the Change Date, the Change License may impose one (e.g. GPL).

citations.note: 'All copies of the original and modified Licensed Work, and derivative works of the Licensed Work, are subject to this License.'
Effective on the Change Date the rights granted shift to the terms of the Change License.
Covenant 1: the Change License must be GPL v2 or later, or a license compatible with GPL v2 or later.

conditions.commentary: During the Business Source period BUSL functions as a strong same-license obligation (s-8): derivatives and redistributed copies stay under BUSL. On the Change Date the license automatically converts to the specified Change License (s-6), which the BUSL covenants require to be GPLv2-or-later-compatible (s-20). BUSL is therefore time-delayed copyleft rather than traditional same-license.

citations.note: The license must be conspicuously displayed on each original or modified copy.
'TO THE EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, THE LICENSED WORK IS PROVIDED ON AN "AS IS" BASIS.'
'LICENSOR HEREBY DISCLAIMS ALL WARRANTIES AND CONDITIONS, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED ...'

limitations.commentary: BUSL uses a combined AS IS / warranty-disclaimer clause. Liability limitation flows from the AS IS basis.

citations.note: 'LICENSOR HEREBY DISCLAIMS ALL WARRANTIES AND CONDITIONS, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING (WITHOUT LIMITATION) WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, NON-INFRINGEMENT, AND TITLE.'
'This License does not grant you any right in any trademark or logo of Licensor or its affiliates (provided that you may use a trademark or logo of Licensor as expressly required by this License).'

limitations.commentary: The license expressly disclaims a trademark grant. Classified as silent on trademark-use-as-a-right because no grant is given; it is not a usage restriction in the sense of a forbidden mark (compare Apache 2.0 §6 which is more explicit about no-endorsement).

sources.source: OSI blog: The Business Source License
